page: Amazon Web Services r8a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R45 processor, which operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, supporting advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. It does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. Small-model LLM text generation is highly efficient, achieving top-tier speeds of 398.29 tokens per second.

Given its high memory density and strong single-core performance, the r8a.2xlarge offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local SSD storage. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, data processing tasks, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, including exceptional single-threaded compression and small-model LLM text generation, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the r8a.2xlarge highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, caching systems, and single-thread-bound database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 AMD EPYC cores, 64.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.2xlarge is an 8-core memory-optimized instance with 64.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, 4 Gbps network bandwidth, and no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining high-speed DDR5 RAM with top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by AWS Nitro
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
